title: malus baccata var. xiaojinensis
intro: Malus baccata var. xiaojinensis, also known as Xiaojin crabapple, is a variety of the Malus baccata species, a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of China. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Xiaojin crabapple are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and possess immunomodulatory effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Xiaojin crabapple is often prepared in herbal decoctions, teas, or infusions, and may also be used in the form of dried fruits, extracts, or tinctures.
